text: Acadia National Park
zh: 阿卡迪亚国家公园

text: day. morning sun sheds light
zh: 清晨,晨光初现

text: on the North American continent. It falls first on the craggy coast of the state of Maine
zh: 照耀在北美大陆上。它最先洒落在缅因州崎岖的海岸

text: and Acadia, the country's most easterly national park.
zh: 以及阿卡迪亚——这个国家最东端的国家公园。

text: A new day is being born. A new life.
zh: 新的一天正在诞生。新的生命正在萌发。

text: In the dawn's hush, people are welcoming the first light.
zh: 在黎明的静谧中,人们迎接第一缕曙光。

text: All that is Acadia. The clean salt air.
zh: 这一切都是阿卡迪亚。清新的海风。

text: The lighthouses. The miles of rocky Atlantic coast.
zh: 座座灯塔。绵延数英里的大西洋岩岸。

text: The verdant forests, fragrant with a scent of pine and spruce.
zh: 苍翠的森林,弥漫着松树与云杉的芬芳。

text: The cool, clear lakes. And the mountains.
zh: 清凉澄澈的湖泊。还有那些山峦。

text: Worn smooth by restless glaciers. A perfect setting for Acadia National Park.
zh: 被不息的冰川磨砺得光滑圆润。这正是阿卡迪亚国家公园的绝佳背景。

text: Located on the rocky coast of down east Maine, the park is home to the tallest mountain on the U .S. Atlantic coast.
zh: 公园坐落在缅因州东部崎岖的海岸,是美国大西洋沿岸最高峰的所在地。

text: All of the reaches of this extraordinary national park begin here,
zh: 这座非凡国家公园的一切延伸,都始于此地,

text: on Mount Desert Island, Arden headquarters of Acadia.
zh: 始于芒特迪瑟特岛,阿卡迪亚的总部所在。

text: The name desert is French, pronounced Douzere, Île de Mont Douzere.
zh: “迪瑟特”这个名字源自法语,读作“杜泽尔”,即“芒特杜泽尔岛”。

text: In this sense, Douzere does not mean a desert island, but rather an island that is deserted, a lonely island.
zh: 在这个意义上,“杜泽尔”并非指荒漠之岛,而是指一座荒芜、孤寂的岛屿。

text: Well, it isn't lonely anymore. Samuel de Champlain, the French explorer,
zh: 不过,它如今已不再孤寂。法国探险家萨缪尔·德·尚普兰,

text: gave the island its name in 1604, after his ship crashed into a shoal.
zh: 在1604年为这座岛屿命名,当时他的船只撞上了一处浅滩。

text: In time, it became home to a scattering of hardy New Englanders.
zh: 随着时间推移,这里成了一批坚韧的新英格兰人的家园。

text: Farmers, lumbermen, shipbuilders, and fishermen plumbing the Atlantic for lobster.
zh: 农夫、伐木工、造船匠,以及在大西洋捕捞龙虾的渔民。

text: a new breed began filtering in, artists of the Hudson River School of Painting.
zh: 后来,一批新的人群开始涌入——哈德逊河画派的艺术家们。

text: Thomas Cole Frederick Church
zh: 托马斯·科尔与弗雷德里克·丘奇

text: Their renderings of beautiful Acadia appeared on magazine covers across the nation.
zh: 他们笔下美丽的阿卡迪亚风光,登上了全美各地的杂志封面。

text: But no artist's palette can do justice to the autumn colors in Acadia National Park.
zh: 但再高超的画笔,也难以完全展现阿卡迪亚国家公园秋日的绚烂色彩。

text: Yet it was to escape the sweltering July and August heat in New York City that the wealthiest of the wealthy began summering in Acadia.
zh: 然而,正是为了躲避纽约酷热难耐的七八月,最富有的富豪们开始来阿卡迪亚避暑。

text: They built storybook mansions, which they modestly called cottages.
zh: 他们建造了如童话般的豪宅,却谦称其为“小屋”。

text: Mount Desert became a playground for the privileged.
zh: 芒特迪瑟特岛由此成了上流社会的休闲乐园。

text: Afternoon teas, strolls down the woods' sylvan paths.
zh: 午后茶叙,林间漫步,穿行在幽静的小径上。

text: Now that Mount Desert Island was a place of pedigree, high society had set out to preserve it.
zh: 随着芒特迪瑟特岛声名远扬,上流社会开始致力于保护这片土地。

text: In time, it became Acadia National Park, America's first national park east of the Mississippi,
zh: 渐渐地,它成为了阿卡迪亚国家公园——美国密西西比河以东的第一座国家公园,

text: and the first anywhere made of entirely donated lands.
zh: 也是世界上第一座完全由捐赠土地建成的国家公园。

text: John D. Rockefeller Jr., trained at Harvard as a landscape architect, undertook the designing of Acadia's carriage roads.
zh: 曾在哈佛接受景观建筑训练的小约翰·D·洛克菲勒,承担了阿卡迪亚马车道的设计工作。

text: He spent 20 years and $20 million on these roads and strictly forbade their use by automobiles.
zh: 他花费二十年时间和两千万美元修建这些道路,并严格禁止汽车通行。

text: Today, park visitors still enjoy these miles of carefully crafted roads,
zh: 如今,公园的游客依然能享受这些精心修建的道路,

text: cyclists, and passengers in horse -drawn carriages.
zh: 骑行者与乘坐马车的游客穿梭其间。

text: activities than taking tea invite today's visitors.
zh: 如今的游客们有比喝茶更丰富的活动可以体验。

text: waters, biking, beaches, hiking,
zh: 戏水、骑行、沙滩、徒步,

text: and boating on the bay. These visitors wade at a shoreline inlet.
zh: 还有在海湾中泛舟。游客们在海岸的一处入海口涉水而行。

text: gushing sea water may splash as high as 40 feet with a roar like thunder.
zh: 汹涌的海水可喷溅高达四十英尺,轰鸣如雷。

text: This is the sight and sound of Acadia National Park.
zh: 这就是阿卡迪亚国家公园的景与声。
